review_questions: Does CP00-149 cover exactly the 40 planned Risk B failure workflow units in order?
Can any CP00-149 row evaluate permission/audit binding, failure taxonomy, execute failure recovery, or write audit/product state?
Can any CP00-149 row emit blocked-claim receipts, Hermes failure evidence, audit failure hints, or write failure fixtures?
Can any CP00-149 row execute failure tests, send Claude prompts, record human escalation notes, render UI, call APIs, or expose hidden failure internals?
Does CP00-149 hand off cleanly from CP00-148 to CP00-150 without implementing LDIP or splitting HRX?

findings.finding: Hermes evidence packet lists docs/closeout-packs/cp00-149/manifest.json in evidence_refs, and both the test and validate-rp03-audit-architecture.mjs read it; the file is not materialized in this changeset. Both readers fall back to docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json (which the passing validation confirms contains CP00-149 with 40 included_units in order), so this is non-blocking. Confirm a materialized manifest is genuinely optional for CP00-149 to match prior-pack parity.

Row case_id is built as <micro_phase>.<behavior_slug> (e.g. rp03_p07_m04.missing_actor_failure) and omits the subphase number, so global uniqueness of the 40 case_ids depends on entry titles being unique within each micro slice (they currently are). Neither validateAuditComplianceCp149Coverage nor the validator asserts case_id/catalog_id uniqueness (only covered-unit-id uniqueness is checked). A future duplicate title within M04 or M05 would silently collapse two rows onto the first in createAuditComplianceCp149FailureWorkflowContinuation without tripping any assertion; a defensive uniqueness guard would harden this.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-149 is a Risk B no-write descriptor pack covering the 40 units RP03.P07.M04.S03-RP03.P07.M05.S20 (M04 S03-S22 secondary-workflow continuation + M05 S01-S20 permission/audit binding continuation). The catalog is purely declarative: every case result is forced no-write through freezeNoWriteResult, and all 56 no-write/no-execute flags are false in both contracts/audit-compliance-contract.json and CP149_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION (no audit/product writes, no permission/audit-binding or failure-taxonomy evaluation, no recovery/retry/rollback/compensation, no blocked-claim/Hermes/audit-hint emission, no fixture writes, no failure test/Claude-prompt/human-escalation/API/network/UI execution, no LDIP, no HRX split; unauthorized counts, object names, and hidden fields are not exposed). Coverage is verified: 40 unique ordered units first=RP03.P07.M04.S03 last=RP03.P07.M05.S20, deliverable_counts {failure_recovery 22, security_audit 4, implementation 3, hermes_evidence 4, fixture 2, test 4, claude_review 1}=40, evidence modes 20/20. Plan binding, the CP00-148->CP00-149->CP00-150 handoff (next_subphase RP03.P07.M05.S21, contiguous with last unit S20), H03 evidence and C03 read-only review packets (model claude-opus-4-8, executes_review/sends_claude_prompt false), hidden-field policy, and the schema bump v0.15->v0.16 are all internally consistent, and the validator case lookups (S03/S08/S11/S21 and M05 S01/S20) resolve to the correct catalog_ids. The audit test suite and the RP03 architecture validator both extend coverage for CP00-149 and the stated checks passed. Only two minor P3 maintainability notes; no P0/P1/P2. Production-ready as a descriptor/reference-only closeout pack.
